title: VLM-based Data Filtering for Artist Mesh Creation
teaser: Use modern VLMs to label and filter mesh datasets, building a clean, well-defined corpus that improves training of artist-mesh generation models.
abstract: With the advance of VLMs, they play an increasingly important role in data filtering, processing, and labeling. One key challenge of training models that produce artist-quality mesh topology is the lack of high-quality, unpolluted datasets. This project explores how to use VLMs to define labeling classes for mesh data, prompt or fine-tune VLMs to apply those labels at scale, and then re-train or fine-tune existing mesh generation methods on the filtered dataset to measure whether VLM-curated data leads to measurable improvements in mesh quality. The expected outcome is a small, clean dataset and a reproducible filtering pipeline.
milestones: Define critical labeling classes for mesh data (topology quality, manifoldness, semantic category, defects).
Prompt or fine-tune VLMs to label the dataset and validate label agreement against a small human-labeled subset.
Fine-tune an existing mesh creation method (e.g., TreeMeshGPT) on the filtered dataset and benchmark against the un-filtered baseline.
